title: PSLE 2024 Science (Booklet B)
subtitle: Ministry of Education, Singapore — Primary School Leaving Examination 2024 — Science (0009/2 B).  Booklet B consists of 12 open-ended questions, Q29–Q40 (44 marks).  Total time for Booklets A and B: 1 hour 45 minutes.  For questions 29 to 40, write your answers in this booklet.  The number of marks available is shown in brackets [ ] at the end of each question or part question.  Diagrams have been left blank — click each paste-zone and Ctrl/⌘+V the corresponding image from the original paper.  Open-ended boxes have the same number of writing lines as the answer spaces in the original paper.

elements.text: Booklet B.  For questions 29 to 40, write your answers in this booklet.  The number of marks available is shown in brackets [ ] at the end of each question or part question.  (44 marks)
Question 29.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: State two functions of roots.  [2]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: The diagram shows two plants, E and H.

elements.label: (b)(i)
elements.text: State the difference between the structure of roots of plant E and plant H.  [1]

elements.label: (b)(ii)
elements.text: Plant E grows in a place with little rain.  Explain why plant E has an advantage in such conditions.  [1]
Question 30.  The diagram shows a bird feeding on the nectar of a flower of plant K.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: The bird helps in a process of the reproduction in plant K.  Name this process.  [1]
The diagram shows the life cycle of plant K.

elements.label: (b)(i)
elements.text: Name process R.  [1]

elements.label: (b)(ii)
elements.text: State how dispersal of seeds benefits young plant K.  [1]

Question 31.  Insect P builds its underground nest in a sunny area of a forest.  It carries small pieces of leaves back to its nest for storage.  The diagram shows the area where the leaves are stored.
The young of insect P feeds on fungus L, which grows on the stored leaves.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Explain how the location of the nest helps fungus L in its growth.  [2]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: The eggs of insect P are laid among the stored leaves.  State two benefits for insect P to lay its eggs among the stored leaves.  [2]
Question 32.  Diagram 1 shows two types of plants, F and G, growing in a pond.
A large amount of fertiliser from a nearby farm flowed into the pond, causing the population of plant F to increase.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: As the population of plant F increased, the population of plant G decreased.  Explain why.  [1]
Diagram 2 shows a food web in the pond habitat.

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: How would the population of bird A change when the population of plant F increased?  Explain your answer.  [1]

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: The population of bird B remained the same.  Give two reasons why the decrease in population of plant G caused a decrease in the population of fish C.  [2]
Question 33.  Raj knows that the amount of light can affect the size of tiny openings found on leaves.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: State the function of tiny openings found on leaves.  [1]

Raj carried out an investigation to find out if pollutant S can also affect the size of tiny openings on leaves in three different towns that are located near one another.  His results are shown in the table.

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: State the effect of pollutant S on the size of tiny openings.  [1]

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: To ensure a fair investigation, the size of the tiny openings on leaves in all three towns was measured at the same time.  Explain why.  [1]

Question 34.  Joel investigated the effect of substance X on the eggshell of animal Y.  He placed three similar pieces of eggshell in 40 ml of water in each of tubes A, B and C.  He added different masses of substance X to tubes A, B and C, and recorded his findings after some time.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: State the effect of X on the thickness of the eggshell.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: The female adult animal Y lays its eggs in a nest made of dead plants.  When the dead plants decompose, substance X is produced.  Suggest how this behaviour of adult Y helps the developing animal Y in the egg.  [1]

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: The graph shows the percentage of female and male animal Y that hatch from eggs which are kept at different temperatures.

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: State the temperature at which the eggs of animal Y should be kept to ensure the continuity of its kind.  Explain your answer.  [2]
Question 35.  Hock Seng moved part L to compress a spring for launching a ball as shown in the set-up.  There was no wind.
He measured the distance moved by the ball as shown.  His results are shown below.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: State how the distance moved by the ball changed with the launching angle.  [2]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Name a force acting on the ball at X.  [1]

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: Suggest why it is difficult to measure the distance moved.  [1]

elements.label: (d)
elements.text: Hock Seng repeated the investigation using the same set of launching angles when there was no wind.  He used the same set-up but a similar ball of higher mass.  State a variable that he must keep constant for a fair comparison of distance moved.  [1]
Question 36.  The graph shows how a length of wire made of material S changes with temperature.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: State the length of the wire at 0 °C.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: State the increase in the length of the wire when it is heated from 0 °C to 80 °C.  [1]
Fadil built a model of telephone lines in the day as shown.
During the night, Fadil heard a crack and found that the wires snapped.  There was no wind, and the model was not disturbed by any person or animal.

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: Explain why the wires snapped at night.  [2]

elements.label: (d)
elements.text: Using the same type of wires and equipment, suggest how Fadil can build the model at the same place so that the wires will not snap.  [1]
Question 37.  The freezing points and boiling points of three substances, P, Q and R, are as shown.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: State what freezing means.  [1]
Meimei has a closed container of volume 100 cm³.  She stored 150 cm³ of a substance in the container at 30 °C.

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: State which substance, P, Q or R, she stored in the container.  Explain your answer.  [2]
Meimei has a plastic tray as shown.

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: State if she can store substance Q in the tray at 30 °C.  Explain your answer.  [1]
Question 38.  Mariam set up the circuit in a toy boat as shown.  The metal contacts, A and B, touched the magnet but are not fixed to it.  The magnet is resting on a wooden support.
When Mariam closed the switch, the plastic captain moved up and down continuously.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Explain why the captain moved up and down.  [2]
Mariam played with the toy for a few hours.  The captain moved slower than before.

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: State another change to the movement of the captain.  Explain your answer.  [2]
Question 39.  A boy has his height measured with a measuring tape as shown.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text:
State the reading on the measuring tape.  [1]

Reading: ____________

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Without using other apparatus and materials, describe one way to obtain an accurate measurement of his height.  [1]
Question 40.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Carol glued two types of blocks of the same size, A and B, and placed them in water as shown.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Based on the diagram, state the difference in a physical property between the two types of blocks.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Carol next placed another two blocks of the same size in water as shown.

elements.label: (b)
elements.text:
She concluded that the two blocks are made of the same material.

State if you agree with Carol's conclusion.  Explain your answer.  [1]
